State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

May 25, 1978

Senator John Garamendi
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Senator Garamendi:

Thank you for your recent letter in which you ask about
a postage meter imprinted with Superintendent of Public
Instruction Wilson Files' signature and message.

We called the office of the Superintendent for further
information and were told by an attorney in his office that
the postage meter imprint has been discontinued.

The use of a public official's name on mailings to the
public after the official has filed for office is regulated
by Government Code Section 89001 and Commission Regulation
18901. The Commission has also issued a recent opinion to
the general counsel for Secretary of State March Fong Eu.
Copies of the regulation and opinion are enclosed.

The question of a signature and a message on an envelope
within which there are different enclosures has never been
addressed by the Commission. Since this practice has been
discontinued, we feel that no further action is necessary.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:hc

SUBCOMMITTEE ON SOCIAL
SERVICES AND WELFARE

Senate
California Legislature '18
APR 26

JOHN GARAMENDI
SENATOR

ALPINE, AMADOR, CALAVERAS, EL DORADO, MONO, SACRAMENTO,
SAN JOAQUIN, STANISLAUS AND TUOLUMNE COUNTIES

April 26, 1978

Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

     Enclosed is an envelop that was sent to a constituent
of mine. Several comments and questions are raised by
Mrs. Garamendi and her fellow teachers concerning the use
of a stamp by the Department of Education.

     I would appreciate your comments on each issue raised
by these people. Thank you for your assistance in this
matter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        John Garamendi

JG:irl

Enclosure
